title: Certificate Expiry on API Gateway
root_cause: Certbot renewal cron disabled during maintenance and never re-enabled
resolution: Manually renewed certificate and restored cron job

title: Log Volume Disk Full
root_cause: Debug logging accidentally left enabled in production after troubleshooting session
resolution: Set log level back to INFO; rotated and compressed old logs; freed 45GB

title: Web Server CPU Spike Causing 504 Timeouts
root_cause: Runaway regex evaluation in request validation middleware
resolution: Deployed hotfix to replace catastrophic backtracking regex; restarted nginx workers

title: NFS Stale Mount on Worker Nodes
root_cause: NFS server kernel panic during scheduled patching; clients got stale handles
resolution: Lazy-unmounted stale mounts; remounted after NFS server recovery

title: Network Partition Between AZs
root_cause: Misconfigured security group rule blocked inter-AZ traffic on port range 30000-32767
resolution: Reverted security group change; verified all NodePort services accessible

title: Kubernetes Node NotReady
root_cause: Kubelet crashed due to disk pressure; docker overlay2 consumed all inode space
resolution: Cleaned dangling images and containers; increased inode monitoring threshold

title: Docker Storage Driver Failure
root_cause: Overlay2 filesystem corruption after unclean node shutdown during kernel update
resolution: Rebuilt overlay2 storage; re-pulled affected container images

title: Cache Thundering Herd After Redis Restart
root_cause: All cache keys expired simultaneously after Redis restart; database overwhelmed
resolution: Pre-warmed cache with critical keys; implemented jittered TTL

title: Slow Query Cascade in Order Service
root_cause: Missing index on orders.customer_id after schema migration
resolution: Created btree index; ran ANALYZE on orders table

title: DDoS on Public API Endpoint
root_cause: HTTP flood from botnet targeting /api/v1/search endpoint (200k req/s)
resolution: Enabled Cloudflare Under Attack mode; blocked offending ASN; added rate-limit rule

title: Database Primary OOM Kill
root_cause: Unbounded query result set from missing LIMIT clause in analytics job
resolution: Killed offending query, added LIMIT clause, increased shared_buffers

title: Container CrashLoopBackOff on New Deployment
root_cause: New image referenced non-existent secret for DB_PASSWORD env var
resolution: Created missing Kubernetes secret; pod started successfully on next retry

title: Redis Cache Failure — Session Loss
root_cause: Redis exceeded maxmemory with noeviction policy; all SET commands rejected
resolution: Changed eviction policy to allkeys-lru; flushed stale session keys

title: Auth Service Unreachable
root_cause: LDAP server ran out of file descriptors; all bind requests rejected
resolution: Increased ulimit on LDAP server; restarted slapd daemon

title: Deployment Rollback After Error Spike
root_cause: Breaking database migration in v2.1.0 dropped a NOT NULL constraint needed by API
resolution: Rolled back to v2.0.9; applied corrective migration in v2.1.1

title: GPU OOM on Inference Node
root_cause: Batch inference job allocated more VRAM than available on the GPU node; memory oversubscription triggered OOM killer
resolution: Rescheduled pods with memory limits; added node taint for GPU workloads; monitored via ROCm-smi

title: ROCm Driver Crash
root_cause: ROCm driver encountered an uncorrectable ECC error on MI250 GPU; driver state machine entered fault handler and panicked
resolution: Downgraded ROCm driver from 5.7.1 to 5.6.3; isolated faulty GPU via AMI; coordinated with AMD support for RMA

title: VRAM Allocation Failure
root_cause: Fragmented VRAM across multiple inference containers prevented a large model from loading; hipMalloc returned out-of-memory despite 40% free VRAM
resolution: Rebooted GPU node to defragment VRAM; set pod resource requests for VRAM; enabled ROCm GTT memory fallback

title: Credential Leak in Git History
root_cause: Engineer accidentally committed .env file containing AWS access keys to public Git repository; secret scanner detected within 12 minutes
resolution: Rotated all AWS keys; force-pushed to remove file from history; enabled pre-commit secret scanning; triggered incident review

title: Insider Threat — Data Exfiltration
root_cause: Employee with database read access exported 500k customer records to personal S3 bucket; anomaly detection flagged unusual egress traffic
resolution: Revoked access; isolated the user account; initiated legal hold; engaged forensics team for evidence collection

title: SSRF Attack on Metadata Service
root_cause: Attacker exploited a URL parameter in the document-generation service to query AWS metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254) and retrieved temporary credentials
resolution: Patched input validation to block loopback IPs; rotated instance role credentials; disabled metadata service v1; added WAF rule for SSRF

title: Ransomware on Shared Storage
root_cause: Crypto locker encrypted 12 TB of NFS shares used by multiple teams; ransom note dropped in every directory; backups were partially encrypted due to mounted drive
resolution: Isolated storage array from network; restored from immutable snapshots (6-hour data loss); rebuilt affected NFS clients; implemented air-gapped backup strategy

title: Container Image Vulnerability Exploit
root_cause: CVE-2026-1234 in base image (Alpine 3.18) allowed container escape via a kernel module exploit; attacker gained host-level access on 3 Kubernetes nodes
resolution: Patched all images with updated base; implemented image scanning in CI; blocked containers running as root; enabled seccomp profiles

title: API Key Rotation Failure
root_cause: Scheduled rotation of Stripe API key via automation failed silently due to expired vault token; services continued using revoked key for 4 hours causing 503 errors on payment
resolution: Manually rotated all API keys; fixed vault token renewal pipeline; added monitoring for key rotation success; implemented staged rotation with validation

title: IAM Policy Misconfiguration
root_cause: Overly permissive IAM policy on the CI/CD role granted s3:PutObject to all buckets; a misconfigured pipeline step overwrote production TLS certificate in S3
resolution: Applied least-privilege IAM policy; restored TLS cert from ACM; added pipeline guardrails to prevent S3 writes in non-artifact stages

title: BGP Route Hijack
root_cause: A misconfigured BGP peer advertised a more specific route (/24) for our production IP block (/22) causing traffic to be blackholed via the peer AS
resolution: Disabled the offending BGP session; forced route withdrawal via RPKI; implemented BGP prefix filtering with IRR record validation; rolled out ROV for all peers

title: MTU Mismatch on VPN Tunnel
root_cause: Site-to-site VPN tunnel between datacenter and cloud had MTU 1500 on one end and 1400 on the other; fragmented packets caused TCP retransmission storms
resolution: Standardised MTU to 1400 across all tunnel endpoints; enabled MSS clamping on both sides; verified with ping -M do -s 1472 across all routes

title: DNS Cache Poisoning
root_cause: Attackers injected fake A records for internal services via a vulnerable DNS forwarder; traffic was redirected to attacker-controlled endpoints for 18 min
resolution: Flushed all DNS caches; patched DNS forwarder (CVE-2026-5678); enabled DNSSEC validation; deployed DNS-over-TLS for upstream resolvers

title: Firewall Rule Cascade Failure
root_cause: Network team removed what appeared to be an unused firewall rule but it was implicitly referenced by a stateful rule-group; 200+ production firewall rules stopped matching
resolution: Restored firewall rule from backup; implemented change-management workflow for firewall rules; added automated rule-dependency graph generation before any deletion

title: PostgreSQL Replication Lag Critical
root_cause: A long-running ANALYZE on the primary with aggressive vacuum caused WAL generation to spike; replicas fell behind by 45 minutes; read replicas served stale data
resolution: Cancelled the ANALYZE; monitored replay until replicas caught up; scheduled ANALYZE during maintenance windows; tuned wal_keep_size and replication slots

title: Deadlock Cascade in Order Service
root_cause: Multiple concurrent transactions acquiring locks on orders and inventory in different order caused a deadlock cascade; application retry logic amplified the issue
resolution: Killed blocked processes; implemented consistent lock ordering in application; added deadlock_timeout and retry with exponential backoff

title: WAL Disk Full Preventing Writes
root_cause: Unmonitored WAL archive directory filled the disk (100GB limit); PostgreSQL entered read-only mode; all write transactions failed for 7 minutes
resolution: Manually archived and removed old WAL segments; extended WAL volume to 500GB; set up disk usage alert at 75%; implemented WAL archival to S3

title: Snapshot Backfill Exhausting IOPS
root_cause: Nightly snapshot backfill job for newly provisioned EBS volumes ran concurrently on all 50 new volumes; IOPS quota exhausted causing 800ms write latency on production volumes
resolution: Throttled snapshot creation to 5 concurrent volumes; moved backfill to off-peak hours; provisioned dedicated IOPS for snapshot operations

title: Backup Verification Failure
root_cause: Weekly backup integrity check failed for 3 out of 15 databases; backup files had checksum mismatches due to silent disk corruption on the backup server
resolution: Restored from known-good second copy; replaced failing disks on backup server; enabled Data Integrity Checks (DIC) on all backup volumes; added alert on checksum failures

title: Dead Letter Queue Overflow
root_cause: A malformed message schema from a misconfigured publisher caused all consumer workers to reject messages; DLQ grew to 2M messages in 15 minutes flooding disk
resolution: Stopped the offending publisher; drained DLQ via replay script; added schema validation on both producer and consumer sides; set DLQ max-depth alert

title: Garbage Collection Pause Storm
root_cause: JVM heap reached 90% usage triggering back-to-back Full GCs; each pause lasted 8-12 seconds causing request timeouts and cascading circuit-breaker trips
resolution: Tuned JVM heap from 8GB to 12GB; switched to G1GC with tuned pause target; added GC pause metrics to Prometheus; implemented heap dumps on OOM

title: Inter-Service Deadlock
root_cause: Service A holds lock on resource X and waits for resource Y; Service B holds lock on resource Y and waits for resource X; both blocked indefinitely
resolution: Terminated both service instances; implemented global lock ordering; added distributed lock timeout with automatic release; added deadlock detection to health check

title: Message Broker Partition Loss
root_cause: Kafka broker lost a replica due to disk failure; unclean leader election caused 2 partitions to lose all committed messages; consumers fell behind by 10M messages
resolution: Restored data from backup; set min.insync.replicas=2; prevented unclean leader election; added partition health monitoring per broker

title: Kernel Panic on Bare-Metal Node
root_cause: Memory ECC error on DIMM slot B2 caused uncorrectable machine check exception; kernel panicked; node dropped from Kubernetes cluster
resolution: Removed node from cluster; scheduled hardware repair; replaced faulty DIMM; ran memtest86 for 24h before restoring to cluster

title: Systemd Unit Drift After Reboot
root_cause: After scheduled reboot, systemd unit file for app-api.service was replaced by an older version from a stale package; service failed to start due to missing environment variable
resolution: Manually restored correct unit file; pinned package versions; added systemd unit diff check in boot-time monitoring; enabled immutable unit file tracking via etcd

title: Prometheus TSDB Corruption
root_cause: Prometheus instance crashed leaving TSDB in an inconsistent state; WAL replay failed; 8 hours of metrics data became unrecoverable
resolution: Deleted corrupted TSDB blocks; rebuilt TSDB from scrape; deployed Thanos sidecar for long-term storage; enabled TSDB integrity checks and alerts
